rulings.Case Title: MELINDA BARANY et al VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Written Discovedry And For Sanctions [Ccp 2030.290, 2033.280, 2031.300]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Written Discovery And For Sanctions [Ccp 2030.290, 2033.280, 2031.300] Granted, in part. Ms. Barany will serve no later than March 22, 2013 verified responses, without objection to: (1) Form Interrogatories, Set One, (2) Special Interrogatories, Set One, and (3) Amended Requests for Admissions, Set One. Monetary sanctions are awarded in favor of Mercury Insurance Company and against Ms. Barany and her counsel in the amount of $ 2,026.50 to be paid no later than April 4, 2013. It is undisputed that the discovery was propounded, and Ms. Barany did not respond with answers or objections. Ms. Barany's objections regarding notice have been addressed adequately by continuation of the hearing from the original date to February 22, 2013, by which time both motions were fully briefed and adequately noticed. To the extent that Mercury Insurance Company requests that the requests for admissions be deemed admitted (CCP § 2033.280(b)), this specific request was not included in the Notice of Motion and is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MELINDA BARANY et al VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, Line 16, PLAINTIFF MELINDA BARANY, ALBERT KUN Mtn For Protective Order Denied. First, the medical report of Jerome Goldstein, M.D., makes no finding of incompetence, but rather a "possible minimal cognitive impairment." (Motion, Kun Decl., Exhibit A.) Even if Ms. Barany is legally incompetent, her medical condition would not excuse her obligation to respond to discovery propounded on her. Second, the discovery requests are not cumulative. Third, the motion is not timely. (CCP § 2033.080(a).) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E PENN et al VS. DIVERSIFIED INVESTMENT AND MANAGEMENT COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Post Judgment Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFFs DENNIS ANDAYA, MARY BACIGALUPI, WILLIAM BLACK, DENISE CASTRO & KEVIN FONG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Post Judgment Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for reasons set forth in plaintiff moving party's papers; defendant Diversified Investment and Management Company does not oppose the motion and has not provided any response to the post-judgment interrogatories and requests for production. Defendant is ordered to provide responses without objections within 30 days. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $885, given no reply papers and assuming no hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. DERMREFRESH SKIN AND LASER MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER BELLONI VS. THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgmenet On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANTS THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, THE CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff's constitutional challenge to the defendant's procedures satisfies the requirement for waste. However, Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient for an inference that she has paid an assessed tax within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 526a. See Cornelius v. Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority (1996) 49 Cal. App. 4th 1761, 1775-78. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure this defect, including adding a party that meets the standing requirements if necessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TERRENCE BARNUM VS. PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order, Sanctions And For Stay Of Proceedings ; Index Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 7, DEFENDANT PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC.'s Motion For Protective Order, Sanctions And For Stay Of Proceedings. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SARINE VOLTAGE VS. SFMTA/MUNI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Special Interrogatories Set One, And Request For Production Set One & For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 8, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Special Interrogatories Set One, And Request For Production Set One & For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for reasons set forth in defendant moving party's papers. Plaintiff Sarine Voltage is ordered to serve complete verified responses, without objection, to defendant's special interrogatories and requests for production of documents within 30 days. Plaintiff is further ordered to serve complete verified responses, without objection, to form interrogatories 6.1 through 6.7, 8.1 through 8.8, and 10.1 through 10.3, within 30 days. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $400. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK BROWN VS. MAUREEN BARBARA KENNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 4, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT MAUREEN KENNEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead, if he can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to state a cause of action for fraudulent conveyance including what property has been transferred and how. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks to have the Court void a pending family law matter, this Court lacks jurisdiction to do so. Plaintiff should file an application for joinder in the family law case under CRC section 5.24. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)
